texto: She stooped for a stone and dropped it down.'Fancy being where that is now,' she said, peering into the blackness; 'fancy going round and round like a mouse in a pail, clutching at the slimy sides, with the water filling your mouth, and looking up to the little patch of sky above.''You had better come in,' said Benson, very quietly. 'You are developing a taste for the morbid and horrible.' ("The Well")
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a W.W. Jacobs: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “She stooped for a stone and dropped it down.'Fancy being where that is now,' she said, peering in…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, drowning, horror pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de horror e well.

frase_original: She stooped for a stone and dropped it down.'Fancy being where that is now,' she said, peering into the blackness; 'fancy going round and round like a mouse in a pail, clutching at the slimy sides, with the water filling your mouth, and looking up to the little patch of sky above.''You had better come in,' said Benson, very quietly. 'You are developing a taste for the morbid and horrible.' ("The Well")

texto: He broke off and eyed with dignified surprise a fine piece of wireless telegraphy between husband and wife. It appeared that Mr. Negget sent off a humorous message with his left eye, the right being for some reason closed, to which Mrs. Negget replied with a series of frowns and staccato shakes of the head, which her husband found easily translatable. Under the austere stare of Mr. Bodfish their faces at once regained their wonted calm, and the ex-constable in a somewhat offended manner resumed his inquiries.
explicacao: Citação de W.W. Jacobs sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “He broke off and eyed with dignified surprise a fine piece of wireless telegraphy between husband…” articula reflexao, humorous, husband em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.
